# Healthcare Operations Handbook

## Patient Triage
Patient triage is the process of prioritizing care based on urgency, severity, and available clinical resources. Effective triage reduces overcrowding, improves patient safety, and supports rapid intervention for life-threatening conditions. Hospitals often use standardized screening questions, vital signs, and escalation protocols to decide who needs immediate treatment.

## Clinical Workflow
Clinical workflow refers to the coordinated sequence of tasks performed by clinicians, nurses, lab teams, and administrative staff. A strong workflow avoids redundant steps, reduces errors, and ensures that each patient receives appropriate care in the correct order. Workflow design often includes handoffs, documentation standards, and escalation triggers.

## Compliance and Governance
Healthcare organizations must comply with privacy rules, clinical documentation standards, and internal oversight policies. Good governance supports a reliable audit trail, reduces legal exposure, and improves patient trust. Compliance programs often include training, periodic reviews, and incident reporting.
